title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 7910 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.

Inspect the Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Discovery Provenance
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-discovery-provenance-1g53qj" that uses case 7986 to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Feed Go Discovery Provenance structural check is only one gate. authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Discovery Provenance, name the reader, publisher, revision, Go, source, tenant, URL, feed format, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, API, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Go Discovery Provenance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Discovery Provenance, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 9720 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted URL, response URL, selected candidate, redirect chain, resolution evidence, and operator decision.

For Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Discovery Provenance, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The final URL alone explains how a source entered the system.
